title: Just Kids
themes: Artistic vocation and the price of creative devotion
The bond between soulmates across romantic and platonic love
Innocence and experience in bohemian New York
Loss, elegy, and the obligation to bear witness
The transformation of poverty into creative crucible
excerpt: Patti Smith's National Book Award-winning memoir of her relationship with photographer Robert Mapplethorpe traces their journey from a chance meeting in 1967 Brooklyn through the hungry years at the Chelsea Hotel to their separate ascents as artists — a tender elegy for a lost New York, a lost generation, and a love that transformed into enduring friendship.
rationale: Book #1 for artists-writers-and-musicians. Root book for an empty subcategory. Just Kids won the National Book Award, the NBCC finalist, and is the definitive artist-memoir of its generation. Chosen to anchor the leaf with a work that embodies the creative partnership between two artists across different mediums — photography and music-poetry — setting the template for subsequent books in this subcategory.
verifiedSources: Wikipedia: Just Kids — ISBN 9780066211312, 278pp, Ecco 2010, National Book Award 2010
